title: uggetc
text: uggetc Move additional data from the current occurrence of the specified entity or current entity into a program buffer. Explain this code short uggetc (unsigned char* entity, unsigned char* buffer, short max); Copy Parameters entity The name of the entity whose data you want to get. A value of NULL denotes the current entity. buffer (Output) The buffer containing the data. max The maximum number of bytes that should be moved. This should not exceed the size of the buffer. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ugget and uggetc Value Meaning >0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer. 0 An error occurred. <0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the field, and that you should call uggetc to get additional data. Use Library: yrtl Description uggetc moves an additional chunk of occurrence data into the specified buffer. This function should be called only after a previous call to ugget or uggetc returned a negative value, to indicate there is more data available. Parameters and return values are the same as for ugget . So if uggetc also returns a negative status, it must be called repeatedly until a positive status is returned. Example: ugget and uggetc The following example illustrates how to use ugget and uggetc to get the data from the current occurrence into a buffer. Because the size of the occurrence is unknown, we read fixed chunks and expand the buffer as necessary. The choice of 100 as the size of the fixed chunks to read is arbitrary. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #define CHUNKSIZE 100 EXPORT void getoccurrence () { char* buf; int len; int totlen; buf = malloc(CHUNKSIZE); /* Allocate the buffer */ len = ugget(NULL, buf, CHUNKSIZE);/* Get the first chunk of data */ totlen = abs(len); /* Set total length */ while ( len < 0 ) /* More to come */ { /* Make room for next chunk */ buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE); /* Read next chunk */ len = uggetc(NULL, buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE); /* Update total length */ totlen += abs(len); } buf = realloc(buf, totlen+1); /* Make room for terminator */ buf[totlen] = 0; /* Add terminator */ } Copy Related Links ugget Move data from the current occurrence of the specified entity or current entity into a program buffer.
